fin: noun. One of the appendages of a fish, used to propel itself and to manoeuvre / maneuver.
finny: adjective. Having one or more fins.
infinity: noun. endlessness, unlimitedness, absence of a beginning, end or limits to size.
inn: noun. Any establishment where travellers can procure lodging, food, and drink.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
nifty: adjective. Good, smart; useful or beneficial, often in an impressively clever way.
nit: noun. The egg of a louse.
tin: noun. A malleable, ductile, metallic element, resistant to corrosion, with atomic number 50 and symbol Sn.
tinny: adjective. Of or pertaining to or resembling tin.
yin: noun. A principle in Chinese and related East Asian philosophies associated with dark, cool, female, etc. elements of the natural world.
finity: noun. The state or characteristic of being limited in number or scope.
iii: numeral. Ⅲ, the Roman numeral three (3).
infit: noun. An inlier - sensitive or information - weighted fit
tiyin: noun. A currency unit of Uzbekistan, one hundredth of a som.
